role_title: Product Manager, Growth AI Outreach Motion

level_signals: Owns revenue motion and defines product vision, strategy, and roadmap
High-visibility role with direct accountability for business outcomes

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of product management experience, taking products from strategy through to shipped outcomes
Experience building AI-powered, conversational, or agentic products. You understand what it takes to ship AI that works reliably for real users, not just in demos.
A track record of owning revenue or business outcomes through product-led approaches
Experience with hypothesis-driven product development and experimentation at scale
Ability to work across existing systems and synthesize them into a coherent product strategy rather than defaulting to net-new builds
Strong written and verbal communication, with the ability to drive alignment across a broad set of stakeholders
An ownership mindset. You do what it takes to solve problems and ship.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with growth problems (acquisition, activation, and onboarding) in B2B or B2B2C environments
Hands-on familiarity with LLMs, conversational AI, or agentic architectures. You have shipped products on top of these technologies, not just evaluated them.
Experience building across channels (chat, email, and voice) with an understanding of the tradeoffs between modalities
Background in scaling high-touch human experiences (sales, support, and product specialists) through technology
Comfortable in high-visibility roles with direct accountability for business outcomes

scope.surface: AI specialist product for self-serve growth motion — inbound chat, voice, email; triage, qualification, onboarding, cross-sell, outbound
scope.business_model: product-led growth, B2B / B2B2C, self-serve and SMB/startup segment
core_responsibilities: Define the AI specialist—what it knows, how it engages, where it draws the line, and when it hands off. Own the product vision, strategy, and roadmap.

Launch and scale the inbound motion across chat, voice, and email for prospects and new users seeking help with products, pricing, and getting started. Drive rapid iteration on answer quality and resolution rates.
Build the triage and qualification layer.
Define escalation paths to the vendor support team and sales team.
Scale past the first interaction into onboarding and cross-sell, leveraging existing Stripe product recommendation, merchant context, onboarding systems, and outreach platform.
Own the revenue this motion produces. Define success metrics and maintain accountability from conversion through expansion, including pipeline generated through AI-qualified leads.
Partner with engineering, AI and ML, design, data science, marketing, sales, and support to execute at pace.

green_flags: Clear product surface and ownership area with specific deliverables described
Strong internal building blocks already exist (product recommendations, onboarding flows, merchant context, outreach infrastructure)
Encourages candidates who meet only minimum requirements to apply
Cross-functional team breadth (engineering, AI/ML, design, data science, marketing, sales, support)
Mission-driven framing with direct revenue accountability
red_flags: No location or work arrangement specified — remote eligibility, hybrid requirements, and cities are all unclear
No compensation or equity information disclosed
High scope ambiguity: role spans inbound, onboarding, cross-sell, and outbound — very broad for a single PM

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years required, scope of ownership, and revenue accountability)
scope.business_model (inferred from team description and self-serve/SMB focus)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed as metrics)
location.type (not stated in JD; set to unclear)
ambiguities: Location and work arrangement (remote, hybrid, onsite) are not mentioned anywhere in the JD body. Treated as unclear.
Visa sponsorship and work authorization are not mentioned. Treated as unclear.
The role straddles Growth PM and AI Product Manager archetypes; Growth PM chosen as primary given team ownership of product-led growth motion, with AI PM as secondary.
